Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.jsonl

Class JsonlTokenWriter

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.marshall.jsonl.JsonlTokenWriter
All Implemented Interfaces:
Closeable, Flushable, AutoCloseable, RecordWriter, TokenWriter
public class JsonlTokenWriter extends Object implements TokenWriter
Reference implementation of the public TokenWriter surface for the JSONL / NDJSON format.

Wraps a JsonTokenWriter and appends a newline after every top-level value emit. The "top-level value" boundary is detected by tracking when an emit returns the cursor to depth == 0.

Notes:
  • This class is not thread safe.

  • Constructor Details

    • JsonlTokenWriter

      public JsonlTokenWriter(Writer out, JsonTokenWriter.Settings settings)
      Constructor.
      Parameters:
      out - The writer to emit JSONL to. Must not be null.
      settings - The output-formatting settings. Must not be null.

  • Method Details

    • forOutput

      public static JsonlTokenWriter forOutput(Object output, JsonTokenWriter.Settings settings) throws IOException
      Internal factory that mirrors JsonTokenWriter.forOutput(Object, JsonTokenWriter.Settings) so the per-format tokenWriter(...) factory can hand in any of the supported output types (Writer, OutputStream, File, StringBuilder).
      Parameters:
      output - The output object.
      settings - The output-formatting settings.
      Returns:
      A new JsonlTokenWriter writing to the resolved underlying writer.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the output type is not supported or could not be opened.

    • binary

      public TokenWriter binary(byte[] value) throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: TokenWriter
      Emits a TokenType.VALUE_BINARY event.

      Text formats normally encode the value as base64-encoded TokenType.VALUE_STRING; the exact encoding is format-defined.

      Specified by:
      binary in interface TokenWriter
      Parameters:
      value - The value. May be null, in which case a TokenType.VALUE_NULL event is emitted instead.
      Returns:
      This object.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred writing to the underlying stream.

    • object

      public TokenWriter object(Object value) throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: TokenWriter
      Emits an arbitrary POJO through the format's structural events.

      The value is walked via PojoWalker: nulls / scalars / collections / arrays / maps / beans are introspected and re-emitted through this writer's TokenWriter.startObject(), TokenWriter.fieldName(String), TokenWriter.string(String), TokenWriter.number(long), TokenWriter.bool(boolean), TokenWriter.nil(), and TokenWriter.binary(byte[]) methods. Per-format encoding (quoting, indentation, newlines, etc.) is whatever this writer is configured for.

      Cursor positioning: the writer must be in a state where a value emit is legal — at root, after a TokenWriter.fieldName(String) call, or inside an array. Calling object(...) from inside an object body without a preceding field name throws IllegalStateException, same as calling TokenWriter.string(String) would.

      Default behavior: the default implementation throws UnsupportedOperationException. Format-specific token writers override this by delegating to PojoWalker.walk(TokenWriter, Object).

      Swaps and @Schema: this method is purely structural. Object swaps and schema annotations are NOT applied at the token layer — use the POJO databind path on Serializer.serialize(Object) for those.

      Specified by:
      object in interface TokenWriter
      Parameters:
      value - The value to emit. May be null.
      Returns:
      This object.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the underlying stream fails.

    • isStreaming

      public boolean isStreaming()
      Description copied from interface: TokenWriter
      Structural token cursors are always O(1)-memory streamers.
      Specified by:
      isStreaming in interface RecordWriter
      Specified by:
      isStreaming in interface TokenWriter
      Returns:
      Always true.

    • close

      public void close() throws IOException
      Description copied from interface: TokenWriter
      Closes the writer, flushing any buffered output first.

      Implementations should be idempotent — closing a closed writer is a no-op.

      Specified by:
      close in interface AutoCloseable
      Specified by:
      close in interface Closeable
      Specified by:
      close in interface RecordWriter
      Specified by:
      close in interface TokenWriter
      Throws:
      IOException
